hard.next_step: 50% confidence 2nd Jul- Proposal for Zycus has been taken to owner of MMC group- Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary and received a positive response and level of interest. Datuk Nural is now working channels to confirm budget tolerance for all 7 ports. Likely 1-2 weeks for responses (including Northport)- the sense is MMC will look at current proposal we have lodged ($240k at Northport) and factor/optimize across all- including small ports and average $150k per port x 7- this remains to be confirmed. Met with Theta Edge - Group CEO on 11th and discussed the proposal in detail.
hard.ais_why: Zycus Opportunity Assessment: Enterprise Transformation Momentum 9.5, AI Readiness 8.8, ERP Modernization 9, Procurement Complexity 9, Supplier Ecosystem Scale 9, Smart Operations Maturity 9, Procurement-Specific Buying Signals 7.5

forecast_read.recommended_forecast: Pipeline, with the close date moved beyond Aug 2026 (realistic close around Nov 2026) pending confirmed group-wide budget tolerance and direct economic-buyer engagement
forecast_read.reason: The Pipeline category itself is honest (Carl Kimball downgraded it from Best Case on 6 Jun 2026), but the 30 Aug 2026 close date is not defensible: budget-tolerance answers across 7 ports are only expected around 16 Jul 2026, leaving about six weeks to complete a group-wide evaluation, negotiation and contracting relay that has not yet started. This is a date problem on a live deal, not a dead deal.
forecast_read.math: 52 days to close (30 Aug 2026 vs today 9 Jul 2026). The deal re-entered Formal Evaluation on 6 Jun 2026 (33 days) after being pulled back from Vendor Selected (1 Jun) as it was restructured from a $240k Northport deal to a $650k 7-port MMC group proposal. The close date has slipped twice (30 Mar to 30 Jun on 28 Apr, then to 30 Aug on 1 Jun). With budget-tolerance responses due about 16 Jul, roughly 6 weeks remain for shortlist confirmation, commercials and contracting across 7 entities, which is not a credible pace.

metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated qualitative pain rather than a quantified business case: on the 25 Nov 2025 call, Nazual Azma Reffin said the need is 'quite urgent' because tendering 'is doing manually' alongside the planned system migration. Internally, Zycus discussed a roughly US$50k-per-port target price (Avoma internal prep call, 30 Dec 2025), since reframed as an average $150k per port across 7 MMC ports (Next Step, 2 Jul 2026), but no ROI or savings figure has ever been validated with the buyer. Quantifying the savings case for the group-wide consolidation, tied to the budget-tolerance discussion now underway, is the key open move.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is now named at the highest level: the MMC group owner (Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary, per Next Step, 2 Jul 2026), who reportedly received the group-wide proposal with 'a positive response and level of interest.' A group-level budget stakeholder (Datuk Nural, role not in Salesforce) is separately working internal channels to confirm budget tolerance across all 7 ports. However, neither has ever appeared on a Zycus-attended call; all engagement with them has been relayed through the partner channel (Zycus met the Theta Edge Group CEO on 11 Jun 2026 to discuss the proposal), so their authority and true sentiment remain unverified by Zycus directly. Securing a direct or partner-brokered confirmation from this office is the critical next step.

decision_criteria.narrative: On the 11 Feb 2026 call the buyer confirmed they want the full procurement suite 'from the MR, part number question... up to the billing side' with automated payment for finance, plus a supplier portal, and asked for a guided hands-on workshop (90-120 minutes) covering the end-to-end Source-to-Pay suite with the AI add-ons and ERP-agnostic integration. Reference validation is also a stated criterion: the buyer asked for site visits to Zycus customers, specifically including a customer whose Zycus deployment is integrated with Ramco. E-bidding and tender control (deposits, award trail, auditability) were the founding criteria from the 25 Nov 2025 call.

decision_process.narrative: The process has escalated through three tiers, visible in the call and Next Step sequence: (1) Northport working level (Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus, Nazual Azma Reffin, Agilaan Praemkumar) drove discovery, demo and proposal through Mar 2026; (2) Northport's new CEO required Agilaan to present 2-3 price comparisons for sign-off (Next Step, 6 Apr 2026), an outcome never confirmed; (3) as of 1 Jun 2026 the opportunity was merged into a group-wide MMC consolidation, taken to the Theta Edge Group CEO (met 11 Jun 2026) and then to the MMC group owner (2 Jul 2026), with a group-level budget stakeholder now confirming tolerance across all 7 ports. Decision authority has moved progressively upward and away from the original Northport contacts.

paper_process.narrative: A concrete paper process existed briefly in Mar 2026: the 17 Mar 2026 S2P proposal, a phased-implementation revision on 27-30 Mar 2026, an LOI target of 31 Mar 2026 and a full BOM/MSA signature target of 7 Apr 2026, with a 1% incremental discount offered per additional port brought in later. Those targets lapsed with no signature. Since the deal moved to the group-wide MMC frame on 1 Jun 2026, no contracting mechanics (MSA scope, legal review, procurement channel, per-port structure) have been re-established for the 7-port proposal. Confirming what paper process applies once budget tolerance clears is unaddressed.

identify_pain.narrative: Northport's core pain is a manual, non-digitized tender and e-bidding process sitting alongside Ramco, which handles purchasing, supplier registration and renewals but has no e-bidding or tender capability. On the 25 Nov 2025 call the buyer said a prior Ramco-brokered Zycus e-bidding collaboration 'never materialized' and asked Zycus directly what it can offer for e-bidding and tender, including tender-fee deposit handling, which needs a custom payment integration. Nazual Azma Reffin called the need 'quite urgent' on the same call. Pain owners: Northport's tender and procurement teams.

champion.narrative: Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus (Head of Tender and Contracts) was a strong, active champion through Q1 2026: per Next Step she and her team were 'strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti Ramco' (10 Dec 2025) and she secured management approval to move forward with Zycus modules (29 Jan 2026). She was last referenced driving the deal in the 12 Mar 2026 Next Step ('follow up our champion for close path'). Since the deal escalated to the group-wide MMC frame on 1 Jun 2026 she has not been mentioned in any Next Step or call, nearly four months without contact, and the current motion runs through the Theta Edge partner channel and the MMC owner's office instead. Her advocacy and access at group level are unconfirmed and she is at risk of being bypassed.
sources: SF Next_Step_History__c (10 Dec 2025, 29 Jan 2026, 12 Mar 2026 entries)

competition.narrative: Ramco is the entrenched incumbent for P2P and purchasing and has actively fought to keep the account: Next Step records it 'pushing them hard' (20 Jan 2026), and Northport management flip-flopped between retaining Ramco P2P short-term (29 Jan 2026) and dropping it ('management for Northport have decided to drop RAMCO now- flip flopped', 9 Feb 2026), with no confirmed resolution since. Competitors__c also lists Ivalua and SAP Ariba, but no call, Next Step or task names either being actively evaluated; they carry no corroborating buyer evidence. The wedge against Ramco is e-bidding and tender capability plus AI-based contract authoring and scoring, which Ramco does not offer.
sources: SF Next_Step_History__c (20 Jan, 29 Jan, 9 Feb 2026 entries)

critical_signals.text: The group-wide proposal reached the MMC group owner on 2 Jul 2026 and drew a positive response, with budget tolerance across all 7 ports now being confirmed and answers expected around mid-July.

On 6 Jun 2026 the deal was rebuilt from a $240k Northport contract into a $650k proposal spanning all 7 MMC ports (about $150k per port), resetting the evaluation and pushing the close to 30 Aug 2026.

The last direct exchange with Northport was Agilaan Praemkumar's 30 Mar 2026 acknowledgement of the revised proposal; over 100 days have passed with no verified buyer reply since, and everything newer is relayed through the partner channel.
Ramco remains the incumbent P2P system and Northport's decision to drop or retain it, which flip-flopped as recently as 9 Feb 2026, has never been confirmed.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A strategically upgraded but currently unverifiable deal: the 7-port MMC consolidation reached the group owner on 2 Jul 2026 with a reported positive response, yet Zycus has had no direct buyer contact since Agilaan's 30 Mar 2026 email, the champion has been silent since 12 Mar 2026, the close date has slipped twice, and the incumbent Ramco question was never closed. Position is early-fight; momentum is running on relayed updates rather than buyer actions.

win_position.text: The proposal reached the MMC group owner on 2 Jul 2026 and drew a positive response and level of interest, giving the deal a path to a group-wide, 7-port decision worth $650k or more (Next Step, 2 Jul 2026).

Northport's tender team was buyer-voiced 'strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti Ramco' (10 Dec 2025) and secured internal approval to proceed with Zycus modules (29 Jan 2026), though that preference read is now over six months old.

PTP, a fellow MMC-group port, is a happy Zycus reference and the differentiators the buyer asked for (e-bidding, AI contract authoring, Ramco-agnostic integration) are gaps Ramco cannot fill (Avoma 25 Nov 2025 and 11 Feb 2026).

No Zycus-verified buyer contact in over 100 days: the last inbound was Agilaan's 30 Mar 2026 proposal acknowledgement, and the Apr 2026 reference-offer emails went unanswered, so the group-level story cannot yet be independently confirmed.

Champion Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus has not been heard from since 12 Mar 2026 and has no confirmed voice in the group-level conversation, leaving Zycus without an inside advocate where the decision now sits.

deal_momentum.text: Forward motion at the top: Theta Edge Group CEO met on the proposal 11 Jun 2026 and the MMC owner received it by 2 Jul 2026 with budget-tolerance responses expected around 16 Jul 2026.
The Next Step trail is fresh and advancing, updated 1 Jun, 6 Jun, 15 Jun and 2 Jul 2026 with a dated forward milestone (budget answers in 1-2 weeks).
Zero direct buyer activity in the scoring window: the last two-way exchange with Northport was 30 Mar 2026, 101 days ago, and no future meeting is booked with any buyer or the group office.
The close date has been pushed twice (30 Mar to 30 Jun on 28 Apr 2026, then to 30 Aug on 1 Jun 2026) and the forecast was downgraded from Best Case to Pipeline on 6 Jun 2026.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer invested real effort through Q1 2026: a management meeting granting in-principle approval (11 Feb 2026 call), a hands-on demo hosted on site (9 Mar 2026), and a working lunch (26 Mar 2026).
Since 30 Mar 2026 no Northport stakeholder has replied, initiated contact or accepted a meeting; the reported owner-level interest (2 Jul 2026) is relayed, not customer-confirmed to Zycus.

deal_risk.text: Budget tolerance across the 7 ports is unconfirmed; the buyer repeatedly flagged budget constraints (ANA was 'loved' but budget-gated, Avoma 30 Dec 2025) and the group may re-price the $650k proposal downward.
The Ramco drop-vs-retain decision flip-flopped (29 Jan vs 9 Feb 2026) and was never resolved; Ramco was actively pushing to protect its P2P renewal (20 Jan 2026).

The 30 Aug 2026 close allows about six weeks after mid-July budget answers to complete a 7-port evaluation, negotiation and contracting relay that has not started, so the date will almost certainly slip.

customer_preference.evidence: The only buyer-voiced preference on record is stale: 'strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti Ramco' (Next Step, 10 Dec 2025, over six months old). The 2 Jul 2026 'positive response' from the MMC group owner is rep-relayed through the partner channel, not buyer-voiced to Zycus, so it cannot carry preference weight in the current window.

business_case.evidence: No quantified ROI or savings case has been validated with the buyer at either the Northport or the 7-port group level; pricing exists (phased proposal, 27 Mar 2026; ~$150k per port frame, 2 Jul 2026) but value quantification does not.

deal_movement.summary: The deal was structurally rebuilt in June 2026 from a $240k Northport contract into a $650k 7-port MMC group proposal, moved back from Vendor Selected to Formal Evaluation to reflect the reset, and escalated through the partner channel to the MMC group owner by 2 Jul 2026.

items.change: Stage moved Vendor Selected to Shortlisted, close date pushed 30 Jun to 30 Aug 2026 as the opportunity was re-framed for the MMC group
Amount raised $240k to $650k, stage set to Formal Evaluation, forecast downgraded Best Case to Pipeline (7-port consolidation)
Zycus met the Theta Edge Group CEO and discussed the group proposal in detail
Proposal taken to the MMC group owner; positive response reported; budget-tolerance confirmation across 7 ports underway

day_summary.overall: The most recent day with real deal movement was 2 Jul 2026, when the deal record was updated to report that the group-wide Zycus proposal had been taken to the MMC group owner and received a positive response and level of interest. Budget tolerance across all 7 ports is now being confirmed through internal MMC channels, with responses expected within one to two weeks. No Zycus-attended buyer meeting occurred; the update is relayed intelligence from the partner channel rather than direct buyer contact.

items.name: Group proposal reached the MMC owner
items.summary: The 7-port proposal was presented to the MMC group owner with a reported positive response; budget-tolerance answers expected by about 16 Jul 2026

competitive_position.summary: Ramco is the single strongest current threat: it is the incumbent P2P and ERP system across the MMC ports, actively pushed back against replacement (20 Jan 2026), and the buyer's stance on retaining versus dropping it flip-flopped as recently as 9 Feb 2026 with no confirmed resolution since. Ivalua and SAP Ariba sit on the Competitors__c field but appear in no call, Next Step or task evidence; treat them as unconfirmed field-only entries pending corroboration. On this sweep no new competitor evidence surfaced, so threat levels are carried forward.

competitors.how_we_win: Lead with e-bidding/tender control and AI-based contract authoring, capabilities Ramco lacks; keep the low-risk Ramco-integration story (APIs for master data, PR/PO, supplier onboarding, PTP precedent) so replacement is additive, not rip-and-replace.

best_practice_check.summary: The deal's biggest levers are re-opening a verified buyer channel before the mid-July budget answers land, restoring the sidelined champion, and closing the Ramco question with a quantified 7-port business case.
flags: Re-engage the Northport working team (Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus, Agilaan Praemkumar) directly: the champion has been dark since 12 Mar 2026 and the deal's only current information channel is partner-relayed.

Convert the MMC owner's reported interest into direct access: broker a Zycus-attended session with the group budget holder via Theta Edge before the ~16 Jul budget-tolerance readout, rather than waiting on relayed updates.

Close the incumbent question with numbers: force a confirmed Ramco drop/retain decision and back it with a quantified per-port savings and integration case so the group prices the 7-port deal on value, not budget gut feel.

Her advocacy is real but stranded at the Northport level.
champion_strength.source: SF Next_Step_History__c (10 Dec 2025, 29 Jan 2026, 12 Mar 2026 entries)

expansion_context.note: No sibling Closed-Won on this account, but PTP (Port of Tanjung Pelepas), a fellow MMC-group port, is a long-standing, renewed Zycus full-S2P customer (Avoma 11 Feb 2026 call) - a group-level credibility and reference asset for the 7-port play.

scope_change.to: $650k consolidated proposal across all 7 MMC ports at an average ~$150k per port
scope_change.detail: On 1-6 Jun 2026 the opportunity was restructured for the MMC group: amount raised 240k to 650k, stage reset from Vendor Selected to Formal Evaluation, forecast moved to Pipeline, close pushed to 30 Aug 2026, and the proposal taken up the group chain to the owner (2 Jul 2026).

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is genuinely in scope and well-positioned: Merlin Intake is on the product list, the buyer asked unprompted how Merlin intake works inside Microsoft Teams (25 Nov 2025), and the team 'loved' ANA in the demo though it was kept optional on budget grounds (Avoma 30 Dec 2025). AIS scores the account 8.0 Strong with AI Readiness 8.8.

ai_fit_signal.summary: The buyer is actively curious about AI (Merlin in Teams, AI-based bid scoring and contract authoring drew direct questions; ANA was 'loved') but budget sensitivity keeps AI modules optional rather than core to the purchase decision.

Until a buyer-side confirmation lands, confidence rests on relayed intelligence.
